latest.title: Manchester NHS Trust transitions MT-RNR1 to BAU
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 7377I
Genedrive PLC
18 June 2026
18 June 2026
genedrive plc
("genedrive" or the "Company")
Manchester University NHS Foundation Trust transitions genedrive's MT-RNR1 test to Business as Usual clinical service
genedrive plc (AIM: GDR), the point of care pharmacogenetic testing company, is pleased to announce that Manchester University NHS Foundation Trust ("MFT") hospitals has transitioned the Genedrive® MT-RNR1 ID kit for the prevention of Antibiotic Induced Hearing Loss in neonates to Business as Usual ("BAU") routine clinical service.
MFT hospitals (St. Mary's, North Manchester and Wythenshawe) together represent approximately 1,800 Neonatal Intensive Care Units ("NICU") admissions annually and have been three of the eight Greater Manchester sites participating in the 14-site nationwide PALOH-UK programme, which aims to address National Institute for Clinical Care and Excellence ("NICE") Real World Evidence generation requirements. To date, more than 30 critically ill babies in Greater Manchester NICUs have been identified with the MT-RNR1 variant, enabling alternative antibiotic prescription and reducing the risk of aminoglycoside-induced lifelong hearing loss. At least one baby carrying the MT-RNR1 variant has been identified at each NICU site in Greater Manchester, reinforcing the case for routine testing across neonatal services.
Dr Ajit Mahaveer, Consultant Neonatologist, Saint Mary's Hospital, Manchester said
:
"The Genedrive assay has become an integral part of our neonatal practice, enabling rapid identification of babies at risk of aminoglycoside-induced hearing loss. Its continued use supports safer, more personalised care and allows timely treatment decisions without delaying antibiotic administration."
Dr Gino Miele, CEO of genedrive plc, said:
"The transition to BAU routine clinical service following PALOH-UK at MFT is a key milestone in driving wider scale adoption of our MT-RNR1 genetic test in the UK. As a Manchester-based company working closely with NHS partners, we are particularly proud that MFT has been central to the development and progression of rapid pharmacogenetic testing in NICUs. Since the introduction of the test, more than 30 babies in Greater Manchester alone have been identified as carrying the MT-RNR1 variant and would otherwise have been at risk of lifelong hearing loss. We are delighted that babies and families served by MFT will continue to benefit from the routine availability of our test.
"We are in active discussions with other hospitals in the PALOH-UK programme to support the transition of our MT-RNR1 test from PALOH-UK based funding to embedding into routine practice to ensure that newborn babies across the UK continue to have access to this preventive test."
Funding for PALOH-UK sites is provided by the Office for Life Sciences("OLS") and National Institute for Health and Care Research ("NIHR") and will cease by June 2026, with some sites completing earlier. Subsequent review and issuance of final guidance by NICE is expected around July 2027.

About genedrive plc (
http://www.genedrive.com
).
genedrive plc is a UK-based, commercial-stage pharmacogenetic testing company focused on rapid, point-of-care diagnostic tests to guide safe and effective drug prescription in emergency and acute care settings.
genedrive's proprietary technology platform enables clinicians to prescribe safer and more effective therapies based on a patient's genetic profile, supporting improved outcomes while reducing pressure on healthcare systems, lowering downstream healthcare costs through the prevention of avoidable complications and earlier, more effective intervention
The Company has two CE-IVD approved and NICE-recommended tests in NHS clinical use. The Genedrive® CYP2C19 ID Kit identifies stroke patients who will not respond to the current standard of care, Clopidogrel, used to reduce risk of secondary stroke. The Genedrive® MT-RNR1 ID Kit helps prevent antibiotic-induced hearing loss (AIHL) in newborns, enabling treatment decisions within actionable timeframes at the point of care.
Headquartered in Manchester, genedrive is focused on scaling UK-developed precision diagnostics within routine care and leveraging real-world evidence to support broader international adoption and commercial growth.

latest.title: Board Changes
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 5629F
Genedrive PLC
26 May 2026
26 May 2026
​
genedrive plc
("genedrive" or the "Company")
Board changes
genedrive plc (AIM: GDR), the point of care pharmacogenetic testing company, today announces that Mr David Nugent has joined the Board as a Non-Executive Director with immediate effect. David has been a shareholder in the Company for several years and more recently was a key cornerstone investor in the successful equity financing announced on 13 February 2026. He owns over 25% of the Company's issued share capital and is the Company's largest shareholder. David has volunteered not to be paid a fee for acting as a Non-Executive Director until the Company becomes EBITDA positive.
Further, Dr. Ian Gilham, who has been a Director of the Company since 2014, including acting as Chairman since 2015, has informed the Board that he anticipates stepping down as Chairman and a Director of genedrive by the end of June 2026. In addition, Chris Yates, who has been a Non-Executive Director of the Company since 2018 has informed the Board that he intends to step down as a Director to focus on his other professional commitments. Chris has agreed to assist the Board during the process to appoint new Non-Executive Board members prior to stepping down sometime over the coming weeks.
The Board is commencing the process to recruit a new independent Non-Executive Chairman as well as two other independent Non-Executive Directors and further announcements will be made in due course.
Dr. Ian Gilham, Chairman of genedrive plc, said:
"On behalf of my fellow Directors I am pleased to welcome David onto the Board today. His support for the Company has been critical to funding our continued growth strategy and we look forward to working closely with him as we execute against our plans to increase the sales and adoption of our two pharmacogenetic tests. Having served as Director of the Company since 2014 it is appropriate that we start the process to find a new Non-Executive Chairman and the Company will update shareholders on this process in due course."
David Nugent, Non-Executive Director of genedrive plc, said on his appointment:
"I would like to thank Ian and Chris for their commitment to genedrive and recognise their significant contribution over many years. I look forward to continuing to work with them as we appoint a Chair and two independent Non-Executive Directors.
"genedrive is in its strongest position for many years, with two point-of-care pharmacogenetic tests now on the market which offer highly promising commercial opportunities. I am excited to be joining the Board and working with Gino and Russ as the Company drives adoption and executes its commercial strategy in the UK and internationally."
Additional Information on the Board Appointment
The following additional information is provided with regards to the appointment of David Grant Nugent, aged 57, in accordance with Rule 17 and Schedule 2(g) of the AIM Rules for Companies:
Current directorships/partnerships:
Former directorships/partnerships (within the last five years):
Newspoint Limited
None
Nugent (SW) Limited
Primeco Limited
Robonet Limited
Roboweb Limited
David Nugent was a director of Howard Nugent Limited which was wound up under the provisions of the Insolvency Act 1986 in March 2011.
David Nugent and his connected parties hold 401,500,000 ordinary shares in the Company as detailed in the TR1 announcement issued on 6 May 2026.
There are no further disclosures required under Rule 17 and Schedule 2(g) of the AIM Rules for Companies.

company_30s.what_they_do: Genedrive makes a handheld device that reads a patient’s DNA in minutes. The test can spot genetic conditions in newborns, guide antibiotic use, and help doctors tailor treatments right at the bedside.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The market for rapid, point‑of‑care genetics is expanding fast. Hospitals want to replace long‑awaited lab results with instant answers, and insurers are willing to pay for tests that cut costs and improve outcomes. Genedrive’s kit is already being trialled by NHS trusts and could scale to other health systems worldwide.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in the early commercial phase. It has completed several NHS pilots, is working on regulatory approvals, and has recently raised capital to keep the pipeline alive. Board changes and a block‑listing return show it is still reorganising its structure while trying to stay afloat.

rns_narrative: NHS pilots are moving forward: the MT‑RNR1 test is being rolled out as a routine service and the CYP2C19 kit is already used in a Bristol trust. These milestones suggest the product is gaining traction in the public‑sector market.
The company is busy with corporate housekeeping—new directors, a fresh equity raise, and a tax‑credit win. These actions aim to shore up cash and signal management’s confidence in the business model.

Regulatory progress is on the horizon: a potential FDA 510k submission is slated for the next few months, and the company has already secured a block‑listing return that could improve its listing status.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism amid a sea of indifference
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders believe NHS adoption and FDA clearance will spark a quick rebound.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others point to the thin capital base, the long path to regulatory approval, and the risk of further dilution as reasons to stay away.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has shifted from almost no discussion to a small cluster of hopeful posts as the NHS pilots begin to report positive results.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat—no public buzz, which leaves the room for a surprise catalyst to hit hard.

near_term_catalysts: Official NHS confirmation that the CYP2C19 kit will be adopted across a wider trust network.
A positive FDA 510k decision or a clear path to approval announced in a press release.
The company’s next equity raise closing, which would shore up cash and potentially lift investor confidence.
Any sign that the block‑listing return has improved the company’s listing status.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in trading volume—at least 5‑10 times the average daily volume—paired with a breakout above the lowest point of the current trading range. A clear announcement of NHS uptake or regulatory approval would also be a green light.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock sits in the early stage of a deep‑crash cycle. APEX compression suggests a tightening price range, but the next move will hinge on confirming that the market has finished capitulating and that a catalyst is about to ignite mean reversion.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The price has fallen far below the levels where the product was first shown to work, yet the underlying technology is gaining real‑world traction. If the stock climbs back to the midpoint of its recent trading range, it could double or triple its current value. The NHS pilots and an FDA decision are the main levers that could trigger that bounce.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Funding remains tight; a regulatory setback or a failure to secure wider NHS adoption could push the price lower still. Further dilution from future equity raises would also erode the current value.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: You could lose the money you invest if the price stays flat or falls further, but if the bounce occurs you stand to make several times that amount. The upside is far larger than the downside, making the trade asymmetrical.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter once the price clears its lowest recent point and volume jumps above the daily average. Use a 5% stop‑loss below the entry and size the position to 10% of your portfolio, taking a profit when the price reaches the upper half of its 52‑week range.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a clear NHS adoption announcement or a positive FDA decision. Monitor daily volume; a spike of 10× the average should prompt a quick entry.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable—there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks identified.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deeply discounted, low‑profile diagnostic company that could rebound sharply once NHS pilots and regulatory approvals unlock its market potential, offering a highly asymmetric upside for those who wait for the mean‑reversion to play out.
